% auto-generated by make_phase5_tables.py -- do not hand-edit
\begin{table*}[!tp]\centering\small
\caption{The configuration decision guide (Phase 5.6): starting priors per axis, with the trade-off inside each rule; ``validate locally'' governs the per-axis priors. Code rows keyed by baseline behaviour (collapse vs not), not model identity.}\label{tab:framework}
\begin{tabular}{p{3.0cm}p{3.4cm}p{10.0cm}}
\hline
Task / context & Recommended & Trade-off in the rule \\
\hline
Short-answer + reference answer & OFF, discriminating model & reasoning gain not significant; OFF 10--925$\times$ cheaper (model-dependent) \\
Code, model that COLLAPSES at 0 (e.g.\ Qwen here) & Reasoning ON (the lever), or switch model & collapse is the explained case: ON recovers QWK on tractable items (Qwen dQWK $+0.136$ full / $+0.113$ V@61) at $\sim$800$\times$ tokens + 2.5--5$\times$ worse SDk; prefer switching to a discriminating model \\
Code, model that does NOT collapse & Default OFF; ON only if local validation justifies it & off the collapse the reasoning gain is inconsistent and not predictable a priori (ns for GLM/DeepSeek-code; sig for GPT$|$RIAYN $+0.192$ and GLM/GPT$|$PT-CS-short) $\to$ OFF by default for cost (116--192$\times$ tokens) + consistency; the unpredictability is itself why you validate locally \\
Consistency/fairness-critical & Reasoning OFF & ON less reproducible (partly length$\times$backend, partly reasoning-intrinsic) \\
Rubric with per-criterion structure & Holistic scoring & criterion-by-criterion mildly hurts agreement with the final grade: small and gold-sensitive ($-0.043$ sig full / $-0.022$ ns verified); consistent with our original distrust of per-criterion scores, which motivated scoring against the final grade \\
Multi-question / session & Clean, question-by-question & shared history $\to$ stricter (p$<$.01); order adds variance \\
Deploy in a new context & Validate locally (governs the above) & transfer is partial: top config carried over, mid-ranking shuffled; unvalidated gold flipped effect readings \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
\end{table*}
